data.title: Stocks gain, FX treads water as markets eye tariffs, Ukraine news
data.description:
Central European stocks firmed on Wednesday while regional currencies were little changed as markets focused on news about Ukraine and the latest developments in tariff wars between the U.S. and its major trading partners.

Budapest's blue chip index led gains, rising 2%. Warsaw equities rose 1.2% while Prague's benchmark index was 0.5% higher.

The European Union will impose counter tariffs on 26 billion euros ($28.37 billion) worth of U.S. goods from April, the European Commission said on Wednesday, in response to blanket U.S. tariffs on steel and aluminum that came into force earlier in the day.

The Polish zloty edged up 0.12% to 4.2 to the euro.

Ukraine said on Tuesday that it was ready to support Washington's proposal for a 30-day ceasefire with Russia. U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio said the U.S. would now take the offer to Russia, and the ball is in Moscow's court.

"Following the news of Ukraine's agreement to a ceasefire this morning, the EUR/PLN exchange rate still remains close to 4.20, but we expect the zloty to strengthen later in the day," Santander Bank Polska wrote in a client note.

Analysts polled by Reuters expect the National Bank of Poland to keep borrowing costs unchanged later in the day, as concerns over elevated inflation remain a focus for policymakers.

Elsewhere, the Hungarian forint firmed 0.16% to trade at 401.05 per euro.

"The forint is trading in the 398-402 range depending on the news flows of the day," an FX trader in Budapest said.

"News on the Ukraine war, tariffs, the latest inflation reading and words of the new (Hungarian) central bank governor are all affecting it, but it would need a stronger trigger to push it out of this range in either direction," the trader said.

Hungary needs sustained tight monetary conditions to curb inflation, central bank Governor Mihaly Varga told a news conference on Wednesday after higher-than-expected February inflation readings that prompted the government to intervene to limit food price rises.

"Varga has reassured markets that he is not looking to cut rates soon, but that may not be good enough for much longer if the current rate of price increase continues," Commerzbank wrote in a note.

data.title: Richmond Hill to Purchase Copper Prospective Mineral Licenses in Canada
data.description:
Richmond Hill Resources (SHNJ.AQ) signed a letter of intent with Three Mile Beach to acquire certain mineral exploration licenses in Quebec, Canada.

The Saint Sophie copper project has more than 14 worked indices and historic mines along with high-grade silver and gold discoveries, according to a Wednesday filing.

The natural resources company will receive a 60-day exclusivity period for due diligence.

data.title: Bund yields hit fresh 17-month high as Merz seeks deal to boost spending
data.description:
Bund yields rose to a fresh 17-month high on Wednesday, as Germany's likely next chancellor worked to secure support for a massive boost in state borrowing aimed at revamping the economy and boosting military spending.

The clock is ticking for Friedrich Merz to persuade lawmakers in the outgoing parliament while a co-leader of the Greens party was non-committal about whether a deal could be done.

Germany's 10-year government bond yields (DE10YT=RR) were up 6 basis points (bps) at 2.931%, the highest level since October 2023. They jumped by 44.7 bps last week in their biggest rise since February 1990.

Markets also await U.S. consumer price data, which could affect expectations for the Federal Reserve's easing cycle.

"That level (0.3% month-on-month core reading) would be too hot and keep at least the front end from pricing a much more aggressive Fed in the near term," ING said in a research note.

Investors appeared to set aside concerns that U.S. tariffs could weaken the economy and prompt the European Central Bank to ease monetary policy, driving borrowing costs lower.

President Donald Trump's 25% tariffs on U.S. steel and aluminium imports took effect on Wednesday.

Citi analysts suggested that if the German fiscal re-pricing moved to the background, shorter-term drivers — such as growth risks from tariffs and global uncertainty — may begin to take over, driving Bund yields to a 2.25-2.75% range.

Traders priced in an ECB deposit facility rate of 2.07% in December (EURESTECBM6X7=ICAP) from 1.92% last week before the announcement of German fiscal plans, and around a 50% chance of a 25 bps rate cut in April.

The spread between 10-year overnight index swap (EUREON10Y=) — regarded as a risk free rate – and Bund yields was at -22.5 bps after hitting -29 bps last week, its lowest level since 2010, during the aftermath of the Global Financial Crisis.

"Despite ongoing turbulence, it is possibly too early to call the end of Bund's safe-haven status," said Jamie Searle, European rate strategy at Citi.

Investors suggested the euro zone safe-haven benchmark might be nearing a tipping point in the asset swap market.

Peace talks remained in focus after Ukraine said it was ready to support Washington's proposal for a 30-day ceasefire with Russia, amid scepticism from Moscow.

Germany's 2-year yield (DE2YT=RR), more sensitive to ECB policy rates, was up 4 bps at 2.24%.

The yield gap between Italian and German bonds (DE10IT10=RR) — a market gauge of the risk premium investors demand to hold Italian debt – was at 104 bps after dropping below 100 bps for the first time since 2021 last week.

data.title: Inflation Bulletin, Trump Talk Lift Wall Street Pre-Bell; Asia Mixed, Europe Up
data.description:
Wall Street futures pointed moderately higher pre-bell Wednesday as traders waited for the latest inflation report from Washington, and after President Donald Trump told business leaders Tuesday there could be tax cuts for domestic production.

The consumer price index (CPI) for February posts at 8:30 am ET, with pundits projecting a 2.9% rise on year, and a 3.2% gain on the CPI-core that strips out food and energy.

On Tuesday, Trump told the Business Roundtable in Washington that tax cuts and regulatory relief were in the cards for US-based manufacturing.

In the futures, the S&P 500 rose 0.6%, the Nasdaq inclined 0.8% and the Dow Jones was up 0.4%.

Asian exchanges traded unevenly overnight, while European bourses tracked moderately higher midday on the continent.

Intel shares rose 8.2% pre-bell on reports that Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing has huddled with US chip designers Nvidia , Advanced Micro Devices , and Broadcom about a venture to own and operate INTC's foundry division.

On the economic calendar, in addition to CPI bulletin, is the EIA petroleum status report at 9:30 am.

In premarket action, Bitcoin traded at $82,233, West Texas Intermediate crude oil traded higher at $66.99, and 10-year US Treasuries offered 4.28%. Spot gold traded for $2,914 an ounce.

data.title: Egypt to lease German gas liquefaction unit to bolster production
data.description:
Egypt, which has struggled with declining domestic gas production, plans to lease a German floating gas liquefaction unit, the Egyptian petroleum ministry said in a statement on Tuesday.

Egypt, the most populous Arab country, has been seeking to ramp up production at its giant Zohr offshore gas field, in a bid to cover rising domestic demand during the summer heat.

Zohr's operator Eni has resumed drilling last month after output dropped in the largest gas field found in the Mediterranean to 1.9 billion cubic feet per day in early 2024, well below the peak reached in 2019.

Egypt's Petroleum Minister Karim Badawi discussed the leasing plans of a unit currently in Germany's Baltic Sea terminal of Mukran with Philipp Steinberg, the German director-general for economic stabilization, energy Security, gas, and hydrogen infrastructure on the sidelines of CERAWeek energy conference held in Houston, Texas.

The two also discussed Germany's possible purchase of Cypriot gas that flows through Egypt's liquefaction facilities for re-export to Europe, the ministry added.

Egypt will send a delegation to Berlin this month to complete the contractual provisions.

Egypt and Cyprus signed agreements last month to enable the export of gas from Cyprus's offshore fields via Egypt as both countries seek to bolster the Eastern Mediterranean's role as an energy hub.

data.title: Lai Si Enterprise Expects to Turn to Profit in 2024
data.description:
Lai Si Enterprise Holding expects a profit after tax between 21 million Macanese pataca and 26 million pataca for 2024, against a loss after tax of 18.7 million pataca from a year ago, a Wednesday Hong Kong bourse filing said.

The construction engineering company attributed the expected turnaround to an increase in revenue and gross profit from its large shopping mall, luxury brand, and retail chain store projects in Macau during the year.

data.title: Moody's sees improved outlook for banks in France, Germany and elsewhere
data.description:
The credit ratings agency Moody's said on Wednesday that it deemed the outlook for banking sectors in France, Germany, Sweden, Belgium and the Netherlands as "stable", an improvement from an early "negative" outlook.

The ratings agency said risks continue as a result of geopolitical tensions, including tariffs.

But the outlook in major banking markets is "now stable, on the back of a lackluster but sustained cyclical economic recovery, bolstered by lower interest rates and stable unemployment," said Effie Tsotsani, a Moody's analyst.

data.title: Novo Nordisk falls on competition concerns from Zealand Pharma-Roche deal
data.description:
** Shares in Novo Nordisk fall around 4% to lowest since July 2023

** Swiss drugmaker Roche secures rights to co-develop obesity drug petrelintide from Danish biotech Zealand Pharma in deal worth up to $5.3 billion

** Nordnet analyst Per Hansen confirms Novo Nordisk share is falling due to new competition from Roche, adding that the deal is "gigantic"

** Novo shares are on track for a 5-day losing streak and are down 18% YTD

** Shares in Zealand Pharma rise about 24% on Wednesday, and Roche is up about 5%

data.title: Draper University and Cardano Forge Partnership to Launch Founder Residency Program in Silicon Valley
data.description:
San Mateo, CA, United States, March 12, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) —

Draper University, a leading accelerator for entrepreneurial talent, and the Cardano Foundation, the organization spearheading the development of the Cardano blockchain, today announced a strategic partnership to launch the Cardano x Draper University Founder Residency Program. This initiative will focus on accelerating the development of Bitcoin decentralized finance (DeFi) solutions, advancing decentralized science (DeSci), revolutionizing the entertainment industry through blockchain, and fostering the creation of user-friendly, mass-market blockchain applications.

The intensive five-week residency, commencing April 14, 2025, at Draper University's Silicon Valley campus, will provide 20 meticulously selected companies with access to premier mentorship, strategic funding, and the resources necessary to realize their innovative visions. Each participating team will receive a 42,000 ADA grant (equity-free) to support their project development.

Program Highlights:

  • Focus on Bitcoin DeFi, DeSci, and Entertainment: The curriculum will explore the transformative potential of blockchain in Bitcoin decentralized finance, decentralized scientific research, and the entertainment sector, emphasizing the creation of novel and engaging applications.
  • Emphasis on Mass Adoption: The program will prioritize the development of user-friendly applications with the potential to reach a broad audience, fostering mainstream adoption of blockchain technology.
  • Expert Mentorship: Participants will benefit from the guidance of seasoned entrepreneurs, blockchain engineers, and industry experts.
  • Strategic Networking: Participants will gain access to a network of investors, industry leaders, and the broader Cardano ecosystem, facilitating strategic partnerships and funding opportunities.
  • Investor Pitch Day: Teams will present their projects to a panel of venture capitalists and strategic investors, seeking seed funding and long-term partnerships.

"We are very excited to partner with Cardano on the Draper University x Cardano Founder Residency and support UTxO-based startups building the future of Bitcoin DeFi." - Tim Draper

This collaboration reflects the shared commitment of Draper University, the Cardano Foundation and Project Catalyst to advance the frontiers of blockchain technology and drive its widespread adoption. By focusing on Bitcoin DeFi, DeSci, entertainment, and mass-market applications, this program seeks to cultivate the next generation of blockchain innovators.

About Draper University: 

Founded in 2012 by Silicon Valley venture capitalist Tim Draper, Draper University is a leading accelerator transforming the way entrepreneurship is taught globally. Through hands-on training, an innovative curriculum, and a focus on thought leadership, Draper University fosters a collaborative environment where young participants, startup founders, executives, and investors can connect and thrive. Draper University boasts a proven track record in Web3 investment, having successfully partnered with leading protocols like Tezos, Algorand, and VeChain, as well as government agencies entering the space.

About the Cardano Foundation:

The Cardano Foundation is an independent, Swiss-based organization dedicated to the growth and development of the Cardano blockchain, a leading third-generation proof-of-stake platform. Committed to fostering a secure and sustainable blockchain ecosystem, the Foundation supports innovation, research, and community engagement.

Application Deadline: March 2025: 

Find more about here or the website https://draperuniversity.com/cardano

Disclaimer: This press release is for informational purposes only and does not constitute financial or investment advice.

Arisa Chelsea Ueno

admissions (at) draperuniversity.com

data.title: Manhattan Bridge Capital 2024 Rev $9.7M >LOAN
Manhattan Bridge Capital 2024 EPS 49c >LOAN
Magnachip Semiconductor Corporation Q4 and Full-Year 2024 Financial Results
data.description:
Magnachip Semiconductor Corporation, a designer and manufacturer of analog and mixed-signal semiconductor platform solutions, has released its financial results for the fourth quarter and full-year 2024. The company has announced a strategic transition to become a pure-play Power company and is exploring all strategic options for its Display business.

Financial Highlights

For the fourth quarter of 2024, Magnachip reported consolidated revenue of $63 million, which was above the mid-point of their guidance range. The consolidated gross profit margin stood at 25.2%, surpassing the high-end of the guidance range. However, the company reported a net loss of $16.3 million for the quarter, translating to a basic and diluted loss per common share of $0.44.

For the full year 2024, consolidated revenues were $231.7 million, a slight increase of 0.7% year-over-year. The Standard Products business saw a 13% year-over-year revenue increase, driven by a 22.5% rise in Mixed-Signal Solutions and a 10.2% increase in Power Analog Solutions. Despite these gains, the company reported a net loss of $54.3 million for the year, with a basic and diluted loss per common share of $1.44.

Business and Operational Highlights

Magnachip's Standard Products business revenue increased by 13% year-over-year, with significant growth in the Mixed-Signal Solutions (MSS) and Power Analog Solutions (PAS) segments. The company also noted a more than 50% year-over-year increase in Power IC revenue. During Q4, Magnachip repurchased approximately 0.7 million shares for an aggregate purchase price of $2.9 million, ending the quarter with $138.6 million in cash.

Strategic Initiatives and Corporate Developments

Magnachip announced its transition to a pure-play Power company and is exploring strategic options for its Display business, which will be classified as discontinued operations starting Q1 2025. The company aims to achieve a quarterly Adjusted EBITDA breakeven by the end of Q4 2025, followed by positive adjusted operating income in 2026 and positive adjusted free cash flow in 2027. This is part of their 3-3-3 strategy, targeting $300 million in annual revenue with a 30% gross profit margin within three years.

Management's Perspective

YJ Kim, CEO of Magnachip, commented on the financial results, stating, "Our Q4 revenue of $63 million was up 24% year-over-year, and gross profit margin of 25.2% was up 2.5 percentage points as compared to a year ago. For the year, Standard Products business revenue increased 13% year-over-year, in line with our guidance for double-digit growth that we provided at the beginning of 2024." Kim emphasized the company's short-term goal of returning to profitability and highlighted the strategic shift to focus on the Power business.

Future Outlook

For Q1 2025, Magnachip expects consolidated revenue from continuing operations to be in the range of $42 to $47 million, reflecting an 8.9% sequential decline due to seasonality but an 11.5% year-over-year increase at the mid-point. The consolidated gross profit margin is expected to be between 18.5% and 20.5%. For the full year 2025, the company anticipates mid-to-high single-digit year-over-year revenue growth and a gross profit margin between 19.5% and 21.5%.

SEC Filing: MAGNACHIP SEMICONDUCTOR Corp [ MX ] - 8-K - Mar. 12, 2025

data.title: Harvard Bioscience Q4 and Full-Year 2024 Financial Results
data.description:
Harvard Bioscience, Inc., a leading developer and manufacturer of life science research tools, has released its financial results for the fourth quarter and full year ending December 31, 2024. The company faced a challenging market environment but showed resilience in several key areas.

Financial Highlights

For the fourth quarter of 2024, Harvard Bioscience reported revenues of $24.6 million, a decrease from $28.2 million in the same period of 2023. The gross margin slightly declined to 57.1% from 58.0% in the previous year. Net income for the quarter was $18 thousand, a significant improvement from a loss of $1.8 million in Q4 2023. Adjusted EBITDA for the quarter was $3.0 million, down from $3.6 million in the prior year.

For the full year 2024, revenues were $94.1 million, down from $112.3 million in 2023. The gross margin for the year was 58.2%, compared to 58.9% in the previous year. The net loss for 2024 was $12.4 million, compared to a net loss of $3.4 million in 2023. Adjusted EBITDA for the year was $7.2 million, a decrease from $14.6 million in 2023.

Business and Operational Highlights

Despite the revenue decline, the company saw strong market reception for its new products, particularly the SoHo™ telemetry systems and MeshMEA™ organoid systems. Pre-clinical revenues strengthened sequentially across all regions, although cellular and molecular technology (CMT) revenues were slightly down due to reduced purchasing by US academic customers.

Strategic Initiatives and Corporate Developments

Harvard Bioscience focused on managing costs to improve operating and financial performance amidst market uncertainties. The company also highlighted its efforts in cost containment and favorable product mix adjustments to mitigate the impact of lower revenues.

Management's Perspective

Jim Green, Chairman and CEO, expressed optimism about the market reception of new products and emphasized the company's focus on cost management to navigate the challenging market environment. He noted the strong interest from academic institutions, contract research organizations, and biopharma customers in the company's latest offerings.

Future Outlook

Looking ahead to Q1 2025, Harvard Bioscience expects revenues to be in the range of $19 million to $21 million, with a gross margin between 56% and 58%. The company remains cautious due to uncertainties in NIH funding and academic research purchasing, along with normal seasonality.

SEC Filing: HARVARD BIOSCIENCE INC [ HBIO ] - 8-K - Mar. 12, 2025

data.title: Clearside Biomedical to Report Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2024 Financial Results and Provide Corporate Update on Thursday, March 27, 2025
data.description:
ALPHARETTA, Ga., March 12, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Clearside Biomedical, Inc.  (“Clearside” or the “Company”), a biopharmaceutical company revolutionizing the delivery of therapies to the back of the eye through the suprachoroidal space (SCS®), announced today that its fourth quarter and full year 2024 financial results will be reported on Thursday, March 27, 2025 after the close of the financial markets. Management will host a webcast and conference call at 4:30 p.m. Eastern Time to discuss the results and provide a corporate update.

The live and archived webcast may be accessed on the Clearside website under the Investors section: Events and Presentations. The live call can be accessed by dialing 888-506-0062 (domestic) or 973-528-0011 (international) and entering conference code: 733956. The Company suggests participants join 15 minutes in advance of the event.

About Clearside Biomedical, Inc.

Clearside Biomedical, Inc. is a biopharmaceutical company revolutionizing the delivery of therapies to the back of the eye through the suprachoroidal space (SCS®) to improve patient outcomes. Clearside’s SCS injection platform, utilizing the Company’s patented SCS Microinjector®, enables an in-office, repeatable, non-surgical procedure for the targeted and compartmentalized delivery of a wide variety of therapies to the macula, retina, or choroid to potentially preserve and improve vision in patients with sight-threatening eye diseases. Clearside is developing its own pipeline of small molecule product candidates for administration via its SCS Microinjector. The Company’s lead program, CLS-AX (axitinib injectable suspension), is in development for the treatment of neovascular age-related macular degeneration (wet AMD). Planning for a Phase 3 program is underway. In addition, Clearside is evaluating various small molecules for the potential long-acting treatment of geographic atrophy (GA). Clearside developed and gained approval for its first product, XIPERE® (triamcinolone acetonide injectable suspension) for suprachoroidal use, which is available in the U.S. through a commercial partner. Clearside also strategically partners its SCS injection platform with companies utilizing other ophthalmic therapeutic innovations. For more information, please visit clearsidebio.com or follow us on LinkedIn and X.

Source: Clearside Biomedical, Inc.

Investor and Media Contacts:

Jenny Kobin

Remy Bernarda

ir@clearsidebio.com

Uranium producers up after Amazon, Google pledge to boost nuclear capacity
data.description:
** Shares of uranium-linked stocks rise premarket after tech majors Amazon.com , Alphabet's Google and shale firm Occidental sign pledge to boost nuclear energy capacity

** On the sidelines of the CERAWeek conference in Houston, companies support the goal of at least tripling nuclear energy capacity by 2050

** Shares of Uranium Energy up 1.8%; U.S.-listed shares of Uranium Royalty and Cameco both rise 1.2%

** Global X Uranium ETF gains 1%

** Nuclear utilities Constellation Energy rises 2.7%, Vistra adds 2.2% and Talen Energy up 1.6%

data.title: Market Talk Roundup: Leonardo Shares Under Pressure Over Lack of Details on Aerostructures Rescue Plan
data.description:
Leonardo's shares came under pressure Wednesday after investors felt disappointed over the Italian aerospace and defense company's failure Tuesday evening to provide a much-expected update on its plans to rescue its ailing aerostructures division. Other issues, including profit taking linked to expectations of a cease-fire deal between Ukraine and Russia, affected the shares. Here is a summary of comments from analysts:

Leonardo's New Targets Could Unlock Further Share Price Boosts

0623 GMT - Leonardo's new targets, through to 2029, are supportive of the recent share-price performance and could unlock further price boosts, Mediobanca analyst Alessandro Pozzi says. The Italian aerospace and defense group's guidance for 2026-28 was higher than the one provided a year ago, while the 2029 guidance is brand new, Pozzi notes. Revenue, Ebitda and free cash flow targets in 2028 are 10% above consensus, he adds. Meeting the 2029 targets, however, would require a meaningful investment in manufacturing capacity, Pozzi writes in a research note. Analysts were expecting a big announcement of a carve-out of Leonardo's aerostructures business, which didn't materialize, he adds. Leonardo's Chief Executive Roberto Cingolani said the company has found a partner to rescue that division but declined to name it, citing confidentiality clauses. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

Leonardo's Aerostructures Rescue Plan Comments Reassure Despite Delay on Details

0757 GMT - Leonardo's much-anticipated update on how it plans to rescue its loss-making aerostructures division is taking longer than expected, after the Italian aerospace and defense group didn't provide details at Tuesday's call with investors, J.P. Morgan analysts David Perry and Lucy Fitzgerald say. Leonardo's Chief Executive Roberto Cingolani said the company has found a partner to rescue that division but declined to name it, citing confidentiality clauses. The analysts say they're quite reassured by Leonardo's comments on this. "If Leonardo can strike a deal that reduces its losses in aerostructures, we think this would be taken very well by investors," they write. Leonardo also said it has set a maximum budget of about 500 million euros this year to make small acquisitions, mainly in the cyber and space domains, the analysts note. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

Potential Ukraine Cease-Fire Raises Prospect of Profit Taking in Defense Sector

0840 GMT - Europe's defense companies face the risk of profit taking in the near-term if Ukraine and Russia reach a cease-fire agreement, after years of consistent share-price increases, Barclays analysts say. Defense has outperformed MSCI Europe by about 60% year-to-date, and investors are now questioning whether multiples have reached bubble territory, the analysts write. Expected good news on rising defense spending is likely priced in now, they say. However, they recommend using potential dips as buying opportunities, given the strong structural growth prospects of European defense companies. Shares in Leonardo are down 3.4%, while Thales falls 6.5% at 228 euros, and BAE Systems loses 2% at 15.44 pounds. Indra Sistemas, QinetiQ, Saab, Dassault Aviation, Fincantieri, Chemring, Babcock International and Hensoldt are also down in morning trade. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

Leonardo's Rating Cut to Hold From Buy By Equita

0909 GMT - Leonardo's rating was cut to hold from buy by Equita, after the Italian aerospace and defense group failed to provide a much-expected update on the future of its loss-making aerostructures division at a call with investors Tuesday. Equita's analyst Martino De Ambroggi said any solution to anticipate break-even would boost shares. He also noted that Leonardo's ambition to make acquisitions in the cyber-space domain "could become expensive given the re-rating of the sector multiples." The company has set aside up to 500 million euros for acquisitions this year. "There is no doubt that Europe is at the beginning of an unprecedented cycle of military investments, but the effects will be diluted over time (timing to be clarified), revisions cannot be ruled out and several countries had already planned an increase," he added. Shares are down 4% at 41.96 euros. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

Leonardo Stock Trading in Fair Valuation Territory

1025 GMT - Leonardo's rating is cut to hold from buy by Kepler Cheuvreux. Its head of Italian equity research, Matteo Bonizzoni, says the Italian aerospace and defense stock is now trading in fair valuation territory, after a 66% rally year-to-date. He also refers to the group's failure to provide a much-awaited update on its plan to rescue its ailing aerostructures division during a call with investors Tuesday, as noted by other analysts. Shares are down 1.8% at 42.96 euros. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

Leonardo's Planned Buyback Program Not an Option for This Year

1035 GMT - Leonardo has indicated plans to launch a buyback program, but BofA Securities analysts Carlos Iranzo and Benjamin Heelan say this won't happen this year. A buyback scheme could still be part of the Italian aerospace and defense company's capital allocation toolkit through 2027, they add. They point out that Leonardo will get about 400 million euros for the sale of its Underwater Armament Systems business and the company's Chief Executive Roberto Cingolani indicated that finding a partner to rescue its aerostructures division won't need a cash injection. Shares are down 2.9% at 42.48 euros. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

data.title: MBA US Mortgage Applications Rise Further in Week Ended March 7, Mortgage Rates Decline
data.description:
Mortgage applications rose by 11.2% in the week ended March 7 on gains in both refinancing activity and new home applications as 30-year fixed mortgage rates fell further, according to Mortgage Bankers Association data released Wednesday.

This follows a 20.4% increase in overall activity in the week ended Feb. 28.

Applications for refinancing loans increased by 16%, while new purchase applications rose by a seasonally adjusted 7%.

The average contract interest rate for 30-year fixed mortgages with loan balances of $806,500 or less fell to 6.67% from 6.73% in the previous week, a sixth straight decline to the lowest level since October 2024.

"As we enter the spring homebuying season, the purchase index was more than 4% higher than a year ago, and activity was up across all loan categories. Government purchase applications experienced an 11% increase - helped by the FHA rate dropping to 6.34%," said Joel Kan, MBA's vice president and deputy chief economist. "Additionally, average loan sizes were higher, with the purchase loan amount hitting $460,800, the highest in the survey dating back to 1990."

data.title: Nabors Files 8K - Asset Acquisition Or Disposition >NBR
data.description:
Nabors Industries Ltd. (NBR) filed a Form 8K - Acquisition or Disposition of Assets - with the U.S Securities and Exchange Commission on March 12, 2025.

The information set forth in the Introductory Note of this Current Report is incorporated herein by reference.

On March 11, 2025, Nabors consummated the Merger and completed the transactions contemplated by the Merger Agreement.

At the effective time of the Merger (the "Effective Time"), each share of common stock of Parker, par value $0.01 per share (the "Parker Common Stock") outstanding immediately prior to the Effective Time was converted into the right to receive (without interest) a pro rata share of the merger consideration, which consisted of up to 4,800,000 Nabors common shares, par value $0.05 per share ("Nabors Common Shares") and a cash payment of $562,000.

The issuance of Nabors Common Shares in connection with the Merger was registered under the Securities Act of 1933, as amended, pursuant to Nabors' Registration Statement on Form S-4 (File No. 3333-282909) (the "Registration Statement"), declared effective by the Securities and Exchange Commission on December 9, 2024.

The foregoing description of the Merger Agreement does not purport to be complete and is qualified in its entirety by reference to the full text of the Merger Agreement, which is filed as Exhibit 2.1 to this Current Report on Form 8-K and is incorporated herein by reference.

The full text of this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1163739/000110465925022754/tm257708d3_8k.htm

Any exhibits and associated documents for this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1163739/000110465925022754/0001104659-25-022754-index.htm

Public companies must file a Form 8-K, or current report, with the SEC generally within four days of any event that could materially affect a company's financial position or the value of its shares.
